```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)
```

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
customers <- vroom::vroom("../input/h-and-m-personalized-fashion-recommendations/customers.csv", 
                          .name_repair = janitor::make_clean_names)
articles <- vroom::vroom("../input/h-and-m-personalized-fashion-recommendations/articles.csv", 
                         .name_repair = janitor::make_clean_names)
transactions_train <- vroom::vroom("../input/h-and-m-personalized-fashion-recommendations/transactions_train.csv", 
                                   .name_repair = janitor::make_clean_names)
sample_submission <- vroom::vroom("../input/h-and-m-personalized-fashion-recommendations/sample_submission.csv", 
                                  .name_repair = janitor::make_clean_names)
```
# Data Overview

## Customers
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
skimr::skim(customers)
```
```{r}
p1 <- customers %>% 
  count(fashion_news_frequency, sort = T) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(fashion_news_frequency, n, fill = fashion_news_frequency)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = F) +
  geom_label(aes(label = scales::comma(n)), fill = "white", size = 3) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1", na.value = "grey50") + 
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma_format()) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = 'Fashion News Frequency', y = '# of customers')

p2 <- customers %>% 
  count(club_member_status, sort = T) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(club_member_status, n, fill = club_member_status)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = F) +
  geom_label(aes(label = scales::comma(n)), fill = "white", size = 3) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1", na.value = "grey50") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma_format()) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = 'Club Member Status', y = '# of customers')

p3 <- customers %>% 
  count(fn, sort = T) %>% 
  mutate(fn = factor(fn)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(fn, n, fill = fn)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = F) +
  geom_label(aes(label = scales::comma(n)), fill = "white", size = 3) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1", na.value = "grey50") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma_format()) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = 'FN', y = '# of customers')

p4 <- customers %>% 
  count(active, sort = T) %>% 
  mutate(active = factor(active)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(active, n, fill = active)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = F) +
  geom_label(aes(label = scales::comma(n)), fill = "white", size = 3) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1", na.value = "grey50") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma_format()) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = 'Active', y = '# of customers')

p5 <- customers %>% 
  count(fn, active, sort = T) %>% 
  mutate(active = factor(active), fn = factor(fn)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(fn, active, fill = n)) +
  geom_tile(show.legend = F, color = "white", size = 2) +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_continuous(type = "viridis", direction = -1) +
  geom_label(aes(label = scales::comma(n)), fill = "white") +
  theme_bw()

p6 <- customers %>% 
  ggplot(aes(age)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = "darkgreen", color = "white") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 5), minor_breaks = NULL) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma()) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "# of customers")
```

```{r}
(p1 + p2) / (p3 + p4)
```
```{r}
p5 / p6
```

**Comments**

- We have 1,371,980 unique customer IDs which corresponds to the number of rows of this dataset
- The `club_member_status` variable has very low missing values, **ACTIVE** is the most dominant category, followed by **PRE-CREATE**
- The `fashion_news_frequency` variable has also low missing value rate (*1%*), **NONE** is the most dominant category, 
followed by **Regularly**. **NONE** might be grouped with **None** and missing values **NA**
- The `fn` variable has a single value **"1"**, 75% of values are missing. My guess is that it is a binary variable.
- The `active` variable has a single value **"1"**, 76% of values are missing. My guess is that it is a binary variable.
- A good portion of customers are young people, 1% of values are missing, could be imputed with median or using other techniques

## Articles

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
skimr::skim(articles)
```

Some variables have many unique values, so let's create a function that calculate the frequency of the top 20 values and display the corresponding bar plots.

```{r}
plot_top20 <- function(dataset, column, column_label){
  dataset %>% 
    count({{column}}, sort = TRUE) %>% 
    mutate(column_reordered = fct_reorder({{column}}, -n, .fun = min)) %>% 
    slice(1:20) %>% 
    ggplot(aes(column_reordered, n, fill = column_reordered)) +
    geom_col(show.legend = FALSE, color = "black") +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma_format()) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = "# of Articles", title = paste("TOP", column_label))
}
```

```{r}
p7 <- plot_top20(articles, product_type_name, "Product Types") +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(direction = -1)

p8 <- plot_top20(articles, product_group_name, "Product Groups") +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "magma", direction = -1)

p9 <- plot_top20(articles, department_name, "Departments") +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "inferno", direction = -1)

p10 <- plot_top20(articles, index_group_name, "Index Groups") +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "plasma", direction = -1)

p11 <- plot_top20(articles, section_name, "Sections") +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "cividis", direction = -1)

p12 <- plot_top20(articles, garment_group_name, "Garment Groups") +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(direction = -1)
```

```{r}
p7
p8
p9
p10
p11
p12
```

Let's see how long are the descriptions of these articles

```{r}
articles %>% 
  transmute(description_length = str_length(detail_desc)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(description_length)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 25, fill = "navy", color = "white") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 800, 50)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma_format()) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "Description Length (# of characters)", y = "# of Articles",
       title = "Distribution of the length of the detailed description of articles")
```

**Comments**

- This dataset has 25 variables, most of them come in (id, name) pairs.
- Numerical variables are IDs that correspond to the associated categorical variables.
- **Trousers, Dresses, Sweaters and T-shirts** are the dominant product types in this dataset.

## Transactions
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
skimr::skim(transactions_train)
```

```{r}
p13 <- transactions_train %>% 
  count(t_dat) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(t_dat, n)) +
  geom_line(color = "red") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma_format()) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "", y = "# of Transactions",
       title = "Transactions Volume Daily Evolution")

p14 <- transactions_train %>% 
  count(t_dat, sales_channel_id) %>% 
  mutate(sales_channel_id = factor(sales_channel_id)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(t_dat, n, color = sales_channel_id)) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma_format()) +
  scale_color_brewer(name = "Sales Channel", palette = "Accent", labels = c("Store", "Online")) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "", y = "# of Transactions",
       title = "Transactions Volume Daily Evolution By Channel")
```

```{r}
p13 / p14
```

```{r}
p15 <- transactions_train %>% 
  ggplot(aes(price)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "brown") +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma_format()) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "Log Price", y = "# of Transactions", title = "Price Distribution")
```

```{r}
p16 <- transactions_train %>% 
  count(customer_id) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(n)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 10, fill = "black", color = "white") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma_format()) +
  xlim(0, 300) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "# of Transactions", y = "# of Customers", title = "Buying Frequency Distribution")
```

```{r}
p15 + p16
```
